title: Calculus 1 - Full College Course
body:

(0:00:00) [Corequisite] Rational Expressions

(8:12:11) Related Rates - Distances

title: Calculus 1 - Full College Course

author: @gkau4337

title: Calculus 1 - Full College Course
body:
This tutorial is amazing but I think there is a error at 6:10:43. I think s(3)= - 9, I graphed the equation and substiuted s= 3 into s(t) and the solution was always - 9. s(t)= t^4 - 16t^3/3 + 6t^2, s(3)= (3^4) - 16(3^3)/3 + 6(3^2)= 81 - 16(27)/3 + 6(9) =81 -16(9) +54= 135 - 144 = -9, if I am wrong I am sorry. Please like so that they are able to see this helpful comment
👇
author: @hrishik207

title: Calculus 1 - Full College Course
body:

title: College Algebra - Full Course
body: I spent a total of 30h over the course of 10 days to finish this crash course and i'll say its 100% worth it for a review of most of highschool algebra and it touches on the foundations of functions and logs. I cannot express enough how much Dr. Linda Green has made math easier for me because of the way she explains these topics, I'm looking forward to finishing the pre-calculus & calclus 1 courses with her for my journey to become a software engineer using the WGU pathway, let's knock out this calculus prerequisite if your doing pre-WGU! Let's GOOOOOO!
author: @freonsp

title: College Algebra - Full Course
body: I love how you are also explaining the logical reasoning behind these basic expressions. That's something that most of us missed in school.

author: @Symbioze

title: College Algebra - Full Course
body:
Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
I have always struggled with math, and am currently taking College algebra. My professor is subpar at most, and the textbook fails to answer my basic questions. I was honestly contemplating dropping out of college, but this simplified version answers the questions that I had. I honestly feel like crying out of gratitude.
author: @damarisiracheta6961

title: College Algebra - Full Course
body: 1:11:03 in Germany we dont say "quadratic formula", we call this the "Mitternachtsformel" (midnight formula), because if the teacher enters our room for creepy or non creepy reasons at midnight while we are asleep and asks us for the formula, we have to know it instantaneously. And I think thats beautiful...
author: @tedp9146

title: College Algebra - Full Course
body: I learn more and more from the net than I do ever did in school. These are passionate people making these videos and the impact is felt deeply. Thank you for being so passionate. This highlights the problem of industrializing education in the modern world where you basically buy yourself a degree and true knowledge or understanding is not sought out. Edexcel, I'm talking to you
author: @aradhyaislam1592

body: 10 minutes in and I'm so proud of myself. Then I realize this is a 6 hour video and I can't stop balling in my chair. 😭

title: Precalculus Course
body:
Errors:
1. 1:24:47
10m should be 20m
2. 1:53:32
The answer to tan φ should be -2.7477 The answer shown was calculated by (cos φ) / (sin φ)
when in fact tan φ should be calculated as (sin φ) / (cos φ)

This is a great course and I am very thankful to have access to it for free. I am only listing these for the purpose of helping others, in case they were confused at these parts :)
author: @johnsmith2931

title: Precalculus Course
body:
I just wanted to say, thank you so much for this free course and I'm really grateful for all the work you put in these videos.
I'm 16 years old and my school system sucks, our math is really bad and without you I don't know if I was going to continue learning math aside from the stupid school system that doesn't help at all.
Books are also a great source of information, I'm very thankful that people like you exist.
author: @miriosdick5682

body: This is so funny I was in college in the mid 1970’s and the only weightlifting equipment at my school was in the football bui! 😂  We ran worked out did yoga but only football players and “muscle heads” used massive weights 😆👍🏼
author: @Georgetown2020

title: Senior Year in 7 Minutes | Year in Review
body: If you're new and finding this video -- hi!! First of all, thank you for watching! Second, my name is Morgan, I'm a recent college grad (as you saw here!), and I've been making YouTube videos for 6 years now. Typically my content is more centered on fashion/lifestyle, but my favorite videos to make are ones like you see here. I'm trying to get to 200K so I'd love if you wanted to subscribe! Again, thanks again for checking out my channel and for all the love on this video.
author: @MorganYatesVids

title: Senior Year in 7 Minutes | Year in Review
body: Legit cried during this like 3 minutes into this Morgan you're a freaking inspiration! Thanks for being the bright, happy and sweetest individual you are! Love ya girl!
author: @leandramontoya2747

title: Senior Year in 7 Minutes | Year in Review
body: I'm a fairly new subscriber, and I cried watching this 😂 you really are taking in every moment of your life and making the most out of it... and I think that's absolutely beautiful and amazing
author: @maddiekrolow3800

title: Senior Year in 7 Minutes | Year in Review
body: Wow. I graduated college 2 years ago and am about to start graduate school. This video has me so inspired to keep a memory journal or something through my time to reflect on. Seriously loved this so much!!  Go Heels, girl. Thanks for always making my day when you upload! You're so real and truly relate to how I feel in the craziness of classes, papers, clinicals, and stressful times!!
author: @courtneysanders3232

title: Senior Year in 7 Minutes | Year in Review
body: I barely even know you, but I got a notification for this video and now I'm legit crying. This is so beautiful

title: Senior Year in 7 Minutes | Year in Review
body: I'm not usually one to comment on any video, but I felt like I couldn't not comment on this beautiful creation. Morgan, I may never meet you but I live all the way in Boston and I have to say I'm so proud of you. To go to an academically challenging school like UNC, and continue to upload youtube videos, and spend time with your friends is truly inspiring. I am going into my senior year (Class of 2018!!) and I truly hope I find a school that fits me like UNC perfectly fits you. I wish you well on all of your future endeavors, no matter how big or how small it may seem to you at that moment. Your content has always brought a smile to my face and I hope that throughout my final year of high school, I can always turn to your videos to make my day. From the bottom of my heart, congratulations. You deserve everything coming your way and I can't wait to see the opportunities you get to experience💙
author: @lovegymnastics77

title: UNC Chapel Hill Pros + Cons | The Truth (my stats!)
body: So here's my parking story... Year's ago (I won't mention how many) when I was faced with the prospect of having to pay a large sum (drinking money) to get a parking permit, I was walking by the university president's residence (William Friday before he passed away) and noticed these parking spots in the back of his house. So I looked up his number in a phone book and called his house and asked if I could meet him. He responded by inviting me to his house for lunch where I met his wife and we chatted about my first couple of years experience there at UNC. I then brought up my parking delima and mentioned that I noticed he had extra spaces behind the house, and I asked him if he would mind if I parked my car at his house for the year. He must have been impressed by my bravado, because he happily agreed! Not only did I get to park my car at the President's house for the last two years of my time there, but I also learned a very valuable lesson in life. Have the courage to ask, and the world may occasionally answer you with a magical, "Yes!"
author: @toolmakerone

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: Hey y’all! this vid has been getting a decent amount of views recently so I just wanted to say that yes, my opinion has slightly changed on a few of these topics over the past year after seeing them both with minimal Covid restrictions again. but for the most part, I think most of what I said still stands true. I also have some more experience with some factors that I didn’t mention (ie off campus housing) now, so if you have any questions about anything, feel free to ask and I’ll respond with my updated thoughts!!
author: @ShivNC

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body:
In the 1960s, the state of NC did a very smart thing by assigning major programs of study to the two schools so that that they wouldn't be in competition with each other for funding. This has allowed both universities to pursue their own paths to excellence. I think it's important to understand that NC State is North Carolina's original land grant university with its mission responding to the industrial revolution. This shaped its future toward more practical degree programs compared to classical liberal arts.
Also, NC State wins for ice cream. Howling Cow is legit and it's as fresh and locally sourced as you are going to get. The cows are just a few miles south of campus on a research "farm".
author: @jlpack62

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: One should apply to a department not a school. UNC has some better departments and NCSU has some that are better or different - usually more applied or specialized in Raleigh. I applied for UNC Botany (from a Cornell B.S.) only to realize later NCSU"s Top 5 Horticultural Science Dept. was a better fit for my goals. Both schools partner in some areas like Biomedical Engineering. Also talk to profs who may become your advisors. If you find 2-3 profs in a department that align with your interests and goals, that is probably a good fit. If you can't even find one named, world-class prof then go elsewhere.
author: @LarryHatch

UNC should win for libraries, 7 million volumes vs. 4.3 million for State. I'm an NCSU Masters grad so trying to be objective and factual here. title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body:
Hi! This is such a great video! I am extremely torn between the two and if you could answer some of my questions that would be so helpful! 1. I thought NCSU was better for business (I want to do marketing) because of their many offered programs, Raleigh’s opportunities, and entrepreneurship support. Plus, as I understand, UNC has very few undergrad business degrees, and if you don’t get into Kenyan Flagler your sophomore year, than is it worth risking that as a business major? 2. You might not have experienced this if you graduated before end of 2020 and then all through 2021, but I’ve heard, and read, about many cases of UNC having a toxic environment regarding respecting people’s political opinions…. This is something few talk about but it’s been a big factor in my decision, because it seems to be that UNC’s both students and definitely professors, can be quite stern and biased regarding reading or hearing opposing views… even failing students who write their honest opinions… 3. How important is the prestige at UNC if you want to go the entrepreneurial route, meaning it won’t be a huge factor having UNC on your resume?
Hope you can answer my questions! Thank you.
author: @tayamishin

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body:
A few places I didn’t get to in the video that I think are important:

-Frat Court: pretty self explanatory, a little bit off campus on Western Blvd/Varsity Dr. Looks really nice

-SAS Hall/Park Shops: class buildings on Stinson Dr (underneath the Court of NC), mainly for statistics

-Wolf Village: student apartments that freshmen are allowed to stay in, usually 4bed/2bath units. On the very west of campus.

-PNC Arena/Carter Finley Stadium: obviously where football and basketball teams play. About a 10 minute drive from campus on Trinity Rd
author: @ShivNC

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body: It's funny to think that my old dorm is now one of the honors dorms. I just lived there because it was close to the Design School.
author: @jpack85

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body: Awesome, enjoyed it. Fitts-Woolard Hall has a nice backstory about it's donors. Ed Wollard (an NCSU grad) was Chairman of the Board at Apple when they were failing. He decided he needed to save the company. and was looking for the right CEO. He put on his hat of contrition and called up Steve Jobs and begged him to come back. Jobs had some terms like fire most everyone. Wollard got the Board to do that and the rest is iPhone history. Had Woolard not made that phone call and got the Board to go along with Job's demands....
author: @LarryHatch

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body:
🎯 Key points for quick navigation:

00:23 NC State's campus on Hillsboro Street features student apartments like Valentine Commons and Nelson Hall, home to the Poole College of Management.
01:19 Surrounding the grass field are science-related buildings like Fox and David Clarke, primarily housing labs and research facilities.
02:46 The Brickyard is a central campus area adjacent to Hill Library, a key spot for studying and dining, including options like Chick-fil-A and pizza.
06:06 The Court of North Carolina is a scenic grass field surrounded by academic buildings like Poe, Harrelson, and Caldwell Hall.
09:22 Tally Student Union and Carmichael Gym are major hubs on campus offering dining, recreational facilities, and student services.
15:33 Centennial Campus hosts engineering and textile facilities, including buildings like Hunt Library and Fitzwillard Hall, known for advanced study spaces and labs.
20:37 Main library at NC State emphasizes automated book retrieval over traditional stacks.
21:08 Textiles complex houses key resources for popular textiles major, including classrooms and a scenic view.
21:48 MRC building serves as a major research hub for professors and grad students at NC State.

Made with HARPA AI

title: An HONEST Review of UNC Chapel Hill (From A Recent Grad)
body: I attended UNC Chapel Hill, and UNC is very hooked on "tradition." The campus brings back memories on those campus visits, and major renovations while admittedly would  be an improvement, change will meet some friction here.

title: An HONEST Review of UNC Chapel Hill (From A Recent Grad)
body: Too many grad students teaching math, statistics, psychology and foreign language introductory classes for general college requirements.  It's very hard to get that super high GPA for medical and dental school. It's much easier to get into those schools if you graduate from NC State than UNC-CH.
author: @Lokie-cd2hw

title: An HONEST Review of UNC Chapel Hill (From A Recent Grad)
